Vetryx turns edit check metadata into actionable intelligence.

Built for sponsor CDM, standards, and study start-up teams that need to compare edit check design across studies, programs, and portfolios.

Find what's different

Identify duplicated, inconsistent, or missing edit checks across your portfolio.

See the insights. Take the right actions.

Vetryx gives you portfolio-level visibility—then lets you drill into the detail.

Portfolio-level signals at a glance. Studies, edit checks, review groups, variation rate, and potential coverage gaps — the numbers your team needs before the first meeting.
vetryx.app / intelligence-overview
Every review group, ranked by priority score. Sortable by coverage, similarity, and duplicate density. Click any row to drill into the evidence.
vetryx.app / cluster-review
Field-level evidence for every cluster. See each edit check, which study it came from, its similarity score, and whether it's a duplicate — all in one view.
vetryx.app / cluster-drilldown

Get a snapshot of your data.

Request an Edit Check Snapshot

How it works

Vetryx turns your edit check metadata into insights you can act on.

Step 1

Upload and define your analysis

Upload an ALS / edit check metadata export. Vetryx automatically detects your studies, statuses, check types, and more—so you can define exactly what you want to analyze.

Define your question first. Different combinations answer different questions.

Different combinations, different questions

Change the study, status, and check filters to answer different SSU, standards, and portfolio oversight questions.

Live vs. In-Development Checks

Separate current-state risk from pre-production design decisions.

click to see example insight
Question
How do in-development checks for this study compare to production checks from similar studies?
Example Insight
This in-development study uses 5 logic variations across 8 matched check concepts, while similar production studies show 2 established patterns, indicating design drift before build finalization.

Standard vs. Study-Specific Checks

See where teams reuse, adapt, or recreate existing edit check patterns.

click to see example insight
Question
Are standard checks implemented consistently, or recreated as study-specific across studies?
Example Insight
This check concept is designated as standard, but appears as study-specific in 4 of 7 studies with 3 logic variations, indicating breakdown in standard adoption.

Compare Across Studies

Measure edit check consistency across a program, indication, or portfolio.

click to see example insight
Question
How consistently is this check concept implemented across similar studies?
Example Insight
8 of 10 studies implement this check concept, with 2 missing and 3 using different logic structures, indicating partial coverage and variation.

Prioritize High-Impact Review

Narrow by form, status, source, or criticality to prioritize review effort.

click to see example insight
Question
Which check concepts should be reviewed first based on reuse, variation, and study coverage?
Example Insight
12 high-coverage check concepts appear across 6+ studies with meaningful logic variation, indicating priority targets for review before standards alignment.
Step 1
Upload & define
Upload an ALS / edit check metadata export and define your analysis scope.
Step 2
Focus your scope
Select the studies, statuses, and check types that match your question.
Step 3
Get prioritized insights
Vetryx surfaces key findings and ranks opportunities based on impact and reuse potential.
Step 4
Drill into the details
Explore clusters and drill down to study, form and field level evidence.
Step 5
Act with confidence
Use clear, evidence-backed insights to drive standardization and reduce rework.
